MaxResults.description: The maximum number of brokers that Amazon MQ can return per page (20 by default). This value must be an integer from 5 to 100.

NextToken.description: The token that specifies the next page of results Amazon MQ should return. To request the first page, leave nextToken empty.

tools.name: update_broker
tools.description: Execute the AWS AmazonMQ `update_broker` operation.

region.description: AWS region on which the broker is in

AuthenticationStrategy.description: Optional. The authentication strategy used to secure the broker. The default is SIMPLE.

AutoMinorVersionUpgrade.description:
Enables automatic upgrades to new patch versions for brokers as new versions are released and supported by Amazon MQ. Automatic upgrades occur during the scheduled maintenance window or after a manual broker reboot.

Must be set to true for ActiveMQ brokers version 5.18 and above and for RabbitMQ brokers version 3.13 and above.

Configuration.description: A list of information about the configuration.

EngineVersion.description:
The broker engine version. For more information, see the ActiveMQ version management and the RabbitMQ version management sections in the Amazon MQ Developer Guide.

When upgrading to ActiveMQ version 5.18 and above or RabbitMQ version 3.13 and above, you must have autoMinorVersionUpgrade set to true for the broker.

HostInstanceType.description: The broker's host instance type to upgrade to. For a list of supported instance types, see Broker instance types.

LdapServerMetadata.description: Optional. The metadata of the LDAP server used to authenticate and authorize connections to the broker. Does not apply to RabbitMQ brokers.

SecurityGroups.description: The list of security groups (1 minimum, 5 maximum) that authorizes connections to brokers.

DataReplicationMode.description: Defines whether this broker is a part of a data replication pair.

tools.name: update_configuration
tools.description: Execute the AWS AmazonMQ `update_configuration` operation.

region.description: AWS region on which the broker is in

tools.name: rabbimq_broker_initialize_connection
tools.description:
Connect to a new RabbitMQ broker which authentication strategy is SIMPLE.

            broker_hostname: The hostname of the broker. For example, b-a9565a64-da39-4afc-9239-c43a9376b5ba.mq.us-east-1.on.aws, b-9560b8e1-3d33-4d91-9488-a3dc4a61dfe7.mq.us-east-1.amazonaws.com
            username: The username of user
            password: The password of user

tools.name: rabbimq_broker_initialize_connection_with_oauth
tools.description:
Connect to a new RabbitMQ broker using OAuth. It only applies to RabbitMQ broker which authentication strategy is config_managed.

            broker_hostname: The hostname of the broker. For example, b-a9565a64-da39-4afc-9239-c43a9376b5ba.mq.us-east-1.on.aws, b-9560b8e1-3d33-4d91-9488-a3dc4a61dfe7.mq.us-east-1.amazonaws.com
            oauth_token: A valid access token

tools.name: rabbitmq_broker_get_guideline
tools.description:
Get the general best practices for deploying RabbitMQ on Amazon MQ.

            - guideline_name: It can take the following value:
                - rabbimq_broker_sizing_guide : this guide tells the customer what instance size to pick for production workload
                - rabbitmq_broker_setup_best_practices_guide: this guide tells the customer what are the best practices in setting up the RabbitMQ broker
                - rabbitmq_quorum_queue_migration_guide: this guide tells the customer how to migrate from classic mirror queue to quorum queue
                - rabbitmq_client_performance_optimization_guide: this guide tells the customer how to optimize their application to get peformance gain of using RabbitMQ
                - rabbitmq_check_broker_follow_best_practice_instructions: this contains instruction to check if a given RabbitMQ broker is following best practices

tools.name: rabbitmq_broker_get_cluster_nodes_info

tools.description: Check if the RabbitMQ broker is in alarm.

tools.name: rabbitmq_broker_is_quorum_critical
tools.description: Check if there are quorum queues with minimum online quorum.

tools.name: rabbitmq_broker_get_broker_definition
tools.description: Get the RabbitMQ definitions: exchanges, queues, bindings, users, virtual hosts, permissions, topic permissions, and parameters. Everything apart from messages.
